Israel.name: Lightricks / LTX Studio
Israel.what_they_do: Jerusalem-based creative-AI unicorn behind Facetune, Videoleap, and LTX Studio — a script-to-production AI video platform with persistent character consistency, pro camera controls, and the open-source LTX-2 audio-video model.
Israel.why_relevant: LTX-2 is open-weights and fine-tunable (exactly the LoRA/custom-model stack Lior works in), and LTX Studio needs product analysts and creative technologists who think in scenes, shots, and prompt pipelines rather than single-clip generators.
Israel.signal: Open-sourced LTX-2 in January 2026; $250M ARR as of mid-2025; 520 employees as of April 2026, ongoing product expansion.

Israel.name: Artlist
Israel.what_they_do: Tel Aviv creative-AI platform offering an all-in-one video production suite — AI video, image, voiceover, and newly-launched AI music generation — trusted by 50M+ creators and brands including Google and Microsoft.
Israel.why_relevant: Lior's ex-Artlist background is a direct cultural fit, and the company's 600% new-user surge in Q1 2026 plus the Artlist Studio launch signal an urgent need for AI creative specialists who can bridge the product and the creator workflow.
Israel.signal: $300M ARR reached April 2026 (up from $260M ARR in Jan 2026); launched Artlist Studio April 20 2026 and AI Music powered by Google Lyria 3 on March 26 2026; in talks with investors.

Israel.name: Guidde
Israel.what_they_do: Tel Aviv AI digital-adoption platform that auto-generates step-by-step video guides from recorded workflows, now training both human employees and AI agents on enterprise software.
Israel.why_relevant: Guidde is building the 'video ground truth' layer for agentic AI — a convergence of video production, agentic tooling, and product analytics that aligns with Lior's analyst chops and AI-workflow fluency.
Israel.signal: $50M Series B (oversubscribed) February 2026 led by PSG Equity with monday.com; 3× annual revenue growth for three consecutive years; 100+ employees, aggressively hiring.

Israel.name: Base44 (now part of Wix)
Israel.what_they_do: Israeli vibe-coding platform that turns plain-English prompts into fully functional full-stack web apps — front-end, back-end, auth, and hosting — in minutes, now backed by Wix.
Israel.why_relevant: Base44 is the canonical Israeli vibe-coding product and Wix is now scaling it aggressively; a vibe-coding-fluent AI Creative Specialist with product-analyst skills is exactly who they need to shape use cases, demos, and creative workflows on the platform.
Israel.signal: Acquired by Wix for ~$80M (2025) after viral growth to 250K+ users; Wix is actively expanding the product into 2026 with tighter integrations; 250,000+ users as of early 2026.

Israel.name: AI21 Labs
Israel.what_they_do: Tel Aviv-based AI lab that builds and sells the open-weight Jamba model family (hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture) plus the Maestro agentic orchestration layer for enterprise deployments.
Israel.why_relevant: Direct open-model infrastructure play with a Hebrew-first model angle; Maestro's agentic-planning track needs product analysts and AI solutions engineers who can bridge model capabilities to creative/enterprise use-cases—Lior's Artlist background and LoRA fluency translate directly.
Israel.signal: $300M Series D led by Google & Nvidia, May 2025; Jamba 1.6 launched March 2025; Nvidia acquisition talks reported Dec 2025 ($2–3B range).

Israel.name: Impala AI
Israel.what_they_do: Israeli inference-infrastructure startup whose platform deploys any open LLM (Llama, Mistral, etc.) inside a customer's own VPC at up to 13× lower cost per token, purpose-built for high-volume async agent workloads—document enrichment, synthetic data generation, and content pipelines.
Israel.why_relevant: Impala's explicit target workloads—'synthetic data generation, scheduled content generation, video/image processing'—map directly to the agentic creative pipelines Lior could architect; an AI Solutions Engineer who can demo prompt-to-production workflows on open models is exactly the customer-facing role this seed-stage team needs next.
Israel.signal: $11M Seed led by Viola Ventures & NFX, October 2025; emerged from stealth with Fortune 500 design partners.

Israel.name: Unframe
Israel.what_they_do: Israeli-founded managed AI delivery platform ('The Framery') that wraps model-agnostic open LLM infrastructure and deploys production-grade AI agents into enterprise workflows—document processing, knowledge retrieval, and orchestration—in days rather than months.
Israel.why_relevant: Unframe's open Framery platform and LLM-agnostic stance mean an AI Solutions Engineer with vibe-coding fluency and analyst chops (Lior's profile) can rapidly prototype and ship agent solutions for clients—the exact hybrid role the company is scaling as it opens Tel Aviv R&D headcount post-Series B.
Israel.signal: $50M Series B led by Highland Europe, May 2026; $100M total raised; surpassed $100M in enterprise contracts within 12 months; 70 of 130 employees based in Tel Aviv.

United States.name: Runway
United States.what_they_do: Builds AI video generation and 'general world models' used across film, advertising, and marketing, with a $5.3B valuation and active Hollywood partnerships.
United States.why_relevant: Open roles include AI Engagement Manager, Creative Support Specialist, Design Engineer, and Art Director — a direct match for Lior's ex-Artlist creative-media background and LoRA/model fluency; Artlist and Runway target overlapping creator markets.
United States.signal: Launched $10M Builders fund + Runway Aleph research in March–April 2026; named CNBC Disruptor 50 and Forbes AI 50 in May 2026; ~$860M raised total.

United States.name: fal.ai
United States.what_they_do: Generative media infrastructure platform giving developers API access to 1,000+ image, video, audio, and 3D models with real-time serverless inference.
United States.why_relevant: Fal's platform explicitly supports LoRA fine-tuning and custom model deployment — Lior's Sliding Doors LoRA build is a live demo of their core use case; their 'Applied Machine Learning Engineers' hiring and vibe-coding-friendly SDK position him well.
United States.signal: Raised $140M Series D at $4.5B valuation (Sequoia, Dec 2025); in talks for $300–350M next round at $8B valuation (March 2026); became official partner API for GPT Image 2 (April 2026).

United States.name: Reactor
United States.what_they_do: Developer platform for real-time generative video and world models, providing a unified SDK/API so developers can build interactive AI media applications without managing GPU infrastructure.
United States.why_relevant: Just emerged from stealth (May 28, 2026), is actively expanding headcount, and is targeting film/TV studios — Lior's creative-tech + agentic build skills and video-generation fluency fit the developer-relations and solutions roles a 16-person team at this stage urgently needs.
United States.signal: Raised $59M Series A led by Lightspeed + Jeffrey Katzenberg's WndrCo, announced May 28, 2026; currently 16 employees, scaling fast.

United States.name: Suno
United States.what_they_do: AI music generation platform that lets anyone create original, studio-quality songs from text prompts, with 2M paid subscribers and $300M ARR as of early 2026.
United States.why_relevant: Actively hiring a 'Senior Performance Creative Designer,' Brand & Creative roles, and Data Scientist (Content + Social) — Lior's music-industry DNA from Artlist plus analyst chops make him a rare creative-technical fit at a music-AI company scaling hard post-Series D.
United States.signal: Raised $400M Series D at $5.4B valuation led by Bond Capital (June 4, 2026); hit $300M ARR and 2M paid subscribers (February 2026); WMG partnership announced late 2025.

United States.name: Typeface
United States.what_they_do: Enterprise generative AI platform for on-brand marketing content, orchestrating AI agents, Brand Hub, and campaign workflows for Fortune 500 clients.
United States.why_relevant: Founded by ex-Adobe CTO; actively hiring across product and creative-AI roles; Lior's combination of brand-creative sensibility (Artlist), analyst background, and agentic workflow fluency directly targets Typeface's 'Arc Agents' and campaign-automation product surface.
United States.signal: Cognizant enterprise partnership announced Jan 2026; $165M total raised (Series B, $1B valuation); named Fast Company Next Big Things in Tech 2025; 247 employees as of 2026.

United States.name: Absurd
United States.what_they_do: YC-backed AI brand and performance video studio that produces production-quality ads and brand videos in 72 hours using a multi-agent orchestration layer.
United States.why_relevant: Absurd sits exactly at the intersection of vibe-coding (multi-agent pipelines) and creative output (brand video); their client roster of Hims, Brex, and Replit mirrors the scale-up companies Lior would pitch, and a tiny YC team needs hybrid creative-technologists.
United States.signal: YC-funded (active 2025–2026); videos averaging 400K+ organic views; clients include Hims, Kalshi, Replit, and Brex; production-quality output in 72 hours via agentic orchestration.
